meta.one_liner: Chronic, immune-mediated inflammatory skin disease driven by the IL-23 / IL-17 axis; a mature, biologics-led market now moving toward oral targeted therapy.

headline_stats.label: Approved MoA classes
headline_stats.detail: TNF, IL-12/23, IL-17, IL-23, PDE4, TYK2, AhR, topicals

market.summary: A large, growing, biologics-led market. 2024 estimates cluster around $27–33B and forecasts reach ~$39–51B by the early 2030s (CAGR ~8–11%), though firms differ on scope and methodology. Value is concentrated in IL-23 and IL-17 franchises; growth drivers include the oral-targeted shift, expanded access, and switching — partly offset by TNF and (from 2025) Stelara biosimilar erosion.

attrs.one_liner: Chronic, immune-mediated inflammatory skin disease driven by the IL-23 / IL-17 axis; a mature, biologics-led market now moving toward oral targeted therapy.

attrs.rationale: Selective IL-23 blockade is the current efficacy + durability benchmark in psoriasis.
attrs.mechanism: Anti–IL-23 p19 mAbs starve pathogenic Th17 cells of their maintenance signal.

attrs.name: Interleukin-23 subunit p19
attrs.rationale: Selective IL-23 blockade = best-in-class efficacy + durable, infrequent dosing.
Blocking the effector cytokine gives the fastest, deepest skin clearance.
attrs.mechanism: Neutralise IL-17A (± IL-17F) or the IL-17 receptor.

attrs.name: Interleukin-17A
attrs.rationale: Rapid, high skin clearance; the effector cytokine.
First oral agent to approach biologic-class efficacy; anchors the oral-targeted shift.
attrs.mechanism: Allosteric inhibition of the TYK2 pseudokinase domain blocks IL-23/type-I IFN signalling.

attrs.name: Tyrosine kinase 2
attrs.rationale: Enables oral targeting of the IL-23 pathway with biologic-adjacent efficacy.
Pioneered the IL-23 pathway; now being displaced by selective p19 and facing biosimilars.
attrs.mechanism: Anti-p40 blocks both IL-12 and IL-23.

attrs.name: IL-12/23 subunit p40
attrs.rationale: First IL-23-pathway target (ustekinumab); now bettered by selective p19.
The original psoriasis biologic class; now legacy/value tier in the biosimilar era.

attrs.name: Tumor necrosis factor
attrs.rationale: Legacy target; broad but less skin-selective; biosimilar era.
Holy grail: IL-23-pathway efficacy in a once-daily pill.
attrs.mechanism: Oral peptide competitively blocks the IL-23 receptor.
cons: Phase 3; efficacy vs injectable IL-23 to be proven

attrs.rationale: Convenience/safety tier; oral apremilast and topical roflumilast for milder disease.
attrs.mechanism: Raise intracellular cAMP to dampen pro-inflammatory cytokines.

attrs.name: Phosphodiesterase 4B
attrs.rationale: Oral and topical immunomodulation; modest efficacy, good convenience/safety.
Foundational first-line and steroid-sparing maintenance for mild disease and sensitive sites.
attrs.mechanism: AhR agonism (tapinarof) and vitamin-D-receptor modulation normalise keratinocyte differentiation.

attrs.rationale: Low-cost legacy systemics still widely used globally where biologics are inaccessible.

attrs.positioning: IL-23 leadership (Skyrizi) as its post-Humira immunology anchor; legacy TNF (Humira).
Broadest psoriasis franchise: p40 (Stelara), IL-23 (Tremfya) and the lead oral IL-23R peptide (icotrokinra).
First-mover IL-17 (Cosentyx) with a large rheumatology footprint.
IL-17 (Taltz) plus IL-23 (mirikizumab) expanding from IBD into psoriasis.
Differentiated dual IL-17A/F (bimekizumab) with best-in-class complete-clearance rates.
Oral pathway pioneer with first-in-class TYK2 (Sotyktu).
Next-gen oral TYK2 (zasocitinib) acquired via Nimbus, chasing Sotyktu.
Single-asset IL-17A/F nanobody (sonelokimab) — a differentiated, acquirable clinical-stage story.
Non-steroidal topicals (roflumilast, tapinarof) modernising first-line care.

attrs.note: limited body-surface area; managed with topicals ± phototherapy

attrs.significance: Accelerates share shift toward selective IL-23 and orals.
First oral targeting the best-validated pathway; could redefine the oral segment.
Next-gen oral TYK2 vs Sotyktu benchmark.
Differentiated IL-17A/F nanobody; key value inflection for a single-asset biotech.
